title: Issuance of License for Manning Agencies
content:
Section 1. Requirements for Licensing. Every applicant for license to operate a manning agency shall submit a written application together with the following requirements:
- a. Certified copy of Articles of Incorporation or Partnership registered with SEC, or Certificate of Registration with DTI for single proprietorship.
- b. Proof of financial capacity: verified income tax returns for past 2 years and savings account with minimum balance of P500,000 with authority to examine deposit.
- c. Proof of marketing capability including Special Power of Attorney, manning agreement, crew orders of at least 50 seafarers from new market, and certification from POEA Pre-Employment Services Office.
- d. Clearances from NBI, Anti-Illegal Recruitment Branch of POEA, and other agencies for board members, partners, and proprietors; foreign members require clearance from country of origin.
- e. Verified undertaking to provide seafarers orientation, ensure qualified seafarers, compliance with contracts and laws, insurance coverage, responsibility for claims, joint liability with employer, compliance with labor and maritime laws, responsibility for acts of officials, deployment of at least 50 seafarers within one year, and repatriation.
- f. Joint and several liability undertaking by officers, directors, and partners.
- g. Individual income tax returns for past 2 years.
- h. Proof of bachelor's degree and 3 years business experience for proprietor, partner or CEO.
- i. List of officials and personnel involved in recruitment with appointments, bio-data, photos, and clearances.
- j. Proof of office space of at least 100 square meters.
- k. Proof of publication of application notice.
- l. Certificate of attendance in pre-application seminar.
Only complete applications shall be processed.

Section 2. Payment of Filing Fee. Non-refundable fee of P10,000 upon receipt of complete application.

Section 3. Action Upon Application. Within 15 calendar days, evaluation, inspection, and determination to grant or deny license. Denial forfeits filing fee.

Section 4. Payment of Fees and Posting of Bonds. Upon approval, pay license fee of P50,000, submit escrow agreement of P1,000,000, confirmation of escrow deposit, and surety bond of P100,000. Existing agencies to increase escrow deposit to P1,000,000 within 4 years.

Section 5. Provisional License. Valid for 1 year to deploy 50 seafarers to new principal. Compliance upgrades to full license for 3 years. Non-compliance leads to expiration.

Section 6. Validity of License. Valid for 4 years unless cancelled, revoked, or suspended. Valid only at stated place and for licensed entity.

Section 7. Non-Transferability. License not transferable or assignable. May be extended upon death of sole proprietor for winding up.

Section 8. Change of Ownership/Relationship. Transfer or material change causing dissolution revokes license automatically.

Section 9. Upgrading. Conversion to corporation for upgrading revokes previous licenses automatically.

Section 10. Derogatory Record. License suspended if derogatory record found; appointment of officers/employees may be revoked.

Section 11. Appointment/Change of Officers and Personnel. Subject to prior approval with submission of appointment documents, clearances, and responsibility statements. Changes registered within 30 days.

Section 12. Publication of Changes. Changes in directors, officers, personnel, or revocation/amendment of appointments must be published once in a newspaper and proof submitted.

Section 13. Transfer of Business Address. Requires prior approval with formal notice, board resolution, lease or ownership proof, inspection, and public notice.

Section 14. Establishment of Extension Offices. Subject to prior approval.

Section 15. Renewal of License. Application before expiration with renewed surety bond, escrow agreement, audited financials, clearances, and other requirements. Suspension if equity below minimum until compliance.

Section 16. Monitoring Compliance. Administration monitors undertakings and imposes sanctions for non-compliance.

Section 17. Non-expiration Pending Renewal. License does not expire if renewal application timely and sufficient.

Section 18. Action on Renewal. Within 48 hours, evaluation and inspection to grant or deny renewal. Only complete applications processed.

Section 19. Late Filing. Allowed within 30 days after expiry with P10,000 fine.

Section 20. Escrow Deposit Garnished. Deposit no longer sufficient; agency notified to replenish.

Section 21. Replenishment. Agency must replenish bonds/deposits within 15 days or license suspended.

Section 22. Release of Deposit. Upon voluntary surrender, release after posting surety bond and submitting clearances.

Section 23. Classification, Ranking and Incentives. Administration classifies and ranks agencies and issues guidelines for incentives such as extended validity and express processing.
